direction that the product is taking.

So we kind of propose topics for all of that, and then we go ahead with it.
Usually when we do that, we listed in the new topics for review tab.

So we listed a bunch of topic ideas over here.
So this is the high level of how we've been proposing topics.
In terms of keywords, back when I did the research in Jan and Feb, I didn't actually think a keyword-driven strategy was great for Galileo because you're kind of ahead of Google so much.
So it really was a combination of looking at what conversations were taking place.
So basically scanning, hugging.
This and, you know, forums and that kind of, you know, that crowd basically looking at what was coming up and trying to map it back to Galileo.

So let's go ahead with it.

We would like more feedback, like SME-driven feedback.
For example, Prateek has a ton of knowledge about what's going on in the space, but he doesn't have a lot of time.
So sometimes the comments that he leaves in the articles are very cryptic, and it takes us some time to figure out exactly what he's saying.
And the problem is when we respond with a question or we tag him back, he misses it.
And so there's a bit of a delay in getting that feedback in.
Feedback.
Feedback in general is super important for us, to our internal processes, because any feedback that you give us, we can put it into our flow, and it tailors future articles for that.

So the feedback can be in terms of tone, it can be specific language, so you can literally tell us things like, this is the wrong context, or whatever, it's only applicable in context A, B, C, whatever.
We can put that in as instructions, and future articles will not have that issue.
So every edit that you leave, every piece of feedback that you give is super valuable.
And I think that's been kind of, you know, I don't want to say lacking, but it's just, there hasn't been enough time from Galileo's end to give us that.
I know Aaron leaves a lot of feedback, but it's more like language-oriented, style-oriented, than SME knowledge-oriented.
So, yeah, if you can give us, like, more of that SME knowledge, that would be.
Because then it helps with differentiation as well.
Any, I know I'm talking a lot, but last point, any internal knowledge that you have in terms of docs as well, that'd be great.
Especially stuff like that is very, you know, I don't want to say proprietary data, but stuff that only you guys know.

We're putting that into content, right?
So we can do that.
But for Galileo so far, we've not been able to do it because there's not much bandwidth on the Galileo site.
So that would be some enhancements that would help.
